name: Zhang Fei
description: Zhang Fei, also known as Yide, was a prominent military general serving under the warlord Liu Bei during the late Eastern Han dynasty and early Three Kingdoms period of China. Renowned for his unparalleled bravery, fierce temper, and unwavering loyalty, Zhang Fei is a central figure in the historical novel Romance of the Three Kingdoms, solidifying his legendary status. He was a formidable warrior, often depicted as a large, imposing figure with a booming voice and a fiery personality. His origins are somewhat shrouded in mystery, but his devotion to Liu Bei and his sworn brotherhood with Guan Yu are cornerstones of his character. Zhang Fei participated in numerous battles, consistently displaying exceptional martial prowess and strategic acumen. His most famous feat is arguably his solo stand at Changban Bridge, where he deterred Cao Cao's massive army with his intimidating presence and thunderous roar, allowing Liu Bei's forces and civilians to retreat safely. Despite his military successes, Zhang Fei's impulsive nature and tendency to punish his soldiers harshly ultimately led to his assassination by his own subordinates, Fan Jiang and Zhang Da, who feared his wrath. Nevertheless, Zhang Fei remains a beloved figure in Chinese culture, embodying courage, loyalty, and righteous anger. He is often venerated as a god in Chinese folk religion and is a popular character in opera, film, and other forms of media. His legacy continues to inspire and entertain, solidifying his place as one of the most iconic heroes in Chinese history and mythology.
subtitle: The Yelling General of Unmatched Bravery
achievements: Defeating numerous enemy generals in battle
Sworn brotherhood with Liu Bei and Guan Yu
Protecting Liu Bei on numerous occasions
Instilling fear in the hearts of his enemies

keyMyths.title: The Stand at Changban Bridge
keyMyths.description: Zhang Fei single-handedly held off Cao Cao's advancing army at Changban Bridge with his intimidating presence and earth-shattering roar, allowing Liu Bei's forces to escape. This act cemented his reputation as a fearless warrior. The strategic importance of this act was to allow Liu Bei to regroup his forces and continue the fight.

keyMyths.title: Sworn Brotherhood
keyMyths.description: Zhang Fei, along with Liu Bei and Guan Yu, swore an oath of brotherhood in a peach garden, pledging to serve and protect each other until death. This bond formed the foundation of their alliance. Their bond was a testament to loyalty and brotherhood.

keyMyths.title: Death of Zhang Fei
keyMyths.description: Zhang Fei was assassinated in his sleep by his own subordinates, Fan Jiang and Zhang Da, due to his harsh treatment of his soldiers. His death was a tragic end to a legendary warrior. This event was a result of his impulsive nature and harshness.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A historical novel that romanticizes the events of the late Eastern Han dynasty and the Three Kingdoms period.
sources.text: A historical novel that romanticizes the events of the late Eastern Han dynasty and the Three Kingdoms period.

sources.author: Chen Shou
sources.description: A historical text documenting the history of the Three Kingdoms period of China.
sources.text: A historical text documenting the history of the Three Kingdoms period of China.

associations.name: Bravery
associations.significance: Zhang Fei's courage in battle was legendary, often intimidating entire armies.

associations.name: Loyalty
associations.significance: He remained steadfastly loyal to Liu Bei, even in the face of adversity and potential death.

associations.name: Serpent Spear
associations.significance: His signature weapon, the Serpent Spear, symbolized his ferocity and martial prowess.

associations.name: Tiger
associations.significance: Represents his aggressive fighting style and untamed nature on the battlefield.

associations.name: Fire
associations.significance: Reflects his fiery temper and explosive bursts of anger and energy.

associations.name: Black
associations.significance: Often associated with his imposing appearance and stern demeanor.

associations.name: Brotherhood
associations.significance: His sworn brotherhood with Liu Bei and Guan Yu (the 'Peach Garden Oath') is central to his character.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Both are renowned warriors known for their unmatched combat abilities and fierce tempers.
Share a similar battle rage and are known for fighting with reckless abandon.
Both are exceptionally skilled warriors with a tendency towards uncontrollable rage in battle.
Both are known for their immense strength, loyalty, and roles as powerful warriors in their respective epics.

worshipPractices.name: Temple Worship
worshipPractices.description: Temples dedicated to Zhang Fei can be found throughout China, particularly in regions associated with his life and battles. Worshippers pray for courage, protection, and victory, often offering incense and performing traditional rituals.

worshipPractices.name: Opera Performances
worshipPractices.description: Zhang Fei is a popular character in Chinese opera. Performances often depict his famous battles and heroic deeds, reinforcing his image as a brave and loyal warrior. These performances serve as a form of veneration and remembrance.

cultural.modernLegacy: Zhang Fei remains a symbol of bravery and loyalty in Chinese culture. He is frequently depicted in popular media, including video games, movies, and television shows. His image continues to inspire courage and is often invoked in situations requiring strength and determination. His stories are retold, ensuring his legendary status endures.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Origins
extendedContent.content: While historical records provide limited details about Zhang Fei's early life, the Romance of the Three Kingdoms portrays him as a butcher from Zhuo Commandery, known for his wealth and martial prowess. He is depicted as a boisterous and impetuous man who enjoys drinking wine and making friends. His fateful meeting with Liu Bei and Guan Yu led to the formation of their legendary brotherhood in the Peach Garden Oath. This oath solidified their commitment to restoring the Han dynasty and serving the people. Zhang Fei's initial display of strength and loyalty during their early struggles marked the beginning of his distinguished military career. His background as a commoner contributes to his image as a relatable and accessible hero, contrasting with the aristocratic origins of many other figures in the era. He quickly distinguished himself as a fierce warrior, ready to defend his brothers and their cause with unwavering determination.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests and Battles
extendedContent.content: Zhang Fei participated in numerous pivotal battles during the Three Kingdoms period, solidifying his reputation as a formidable warrior. One of his most famous exploits involves single-handedly holding off Cao Cao's army at Changban Bridge, preventing them from pursuing Liu Bei's fleeing forces. His booming voice and intimidating presence were enough to deter the enemy, showcasing his unparalleled bravery. He also played a crucial role in conquering Yi Province, demonstrating his strategic acumen and military leadership. Zhang Fei's battles were characterized by his aggressive fighting style and willingness to face any challenge head-on. His loyalty to Liu Bei was constantly tested, but he never wavered in his commitment. These quests and battles cemented his place as one of the most celebrated generals of the era, inspiring generations with his courage and unwavering spirit.

extendedContent.title: Legacy and Enduring Symbolism
extendedContent.content: Zhang Fei's legacy extends far beyond the battlefields of the Three Kingdoms. He is celebrated as a symbol of courage, loyalty, and unwavering brotherhood. His image is ubiquitous in Chinese culture, appearing in opera, literature, and modern media. Temples dedicated to him stand as testaments to his enduring influence, where he is venerated as a protector and a symbol of righteous strength. His story continues to inspire, reminding people of the importance of loyalty and bravery in the face of adversity. Zhang Fei is not merely a historical figure; he is a cultural icon whose spirit embodies the values of heroism and unwavering devotion to one's comrades.

shortDescription: A fierce and loyal general under Liu Bei, known for his unmatched bravery and booming voice. A symbol of courage in Chinese culture.
